work_unit: fix recurring reminder ID unbounded -next suffix growth (audit L-004)
situation: ReminderScheduler.check_due() appends '-next' to reminder ID on each recurrence cycle, producing IDs like rm-X-next-next-next... that grow without bound. Each firing also inserts a new row (old row only marked fired=1, never deleted), causing unbounded table growth. For a daily reminder over 1 year: ID ~1825 chars, 365 dead rows.
action: Strip existing -next chain from ID via re.sub(r'(-next)+$', '', id) to recover stable base_id. Use base_id for next occurrence so INSERT OR REPLACE updates the same row in-place with fired=0 and new when_dt. Only mark_fired the old row if base_id != current id (i.e., cleaning up legacy -next rows). Non-recurring reminders unchanged.
outcome_evidence: Committed to opt-personal-assistant-1782909325. re.sub strips -next chain → stable base_id. add_reminder with fired=0 + same base_id triggers INSERT OR REPLACE in storage.py L253-261. Legacy -next rows cleaned via conditional mark_reminder_fired. No behavioral change for non-recurring reminders (else branch preserved).
next_suggestion: L-005 (add_chat_log bare Exception catch → sqlite3.OperationalError specific) is last pending fix for audit-2026-07-02-008. After that, cycle-complete triggers next full audit.
